item.title: Bank of Korea Seen Favoring Hawkish Hold as Inflation Risks Grow
item.description: South Korea’s central bank is widely expected to keep its policy rate unchanged this week, with investors watching for signs policymakers are formally shifting to a hawkish stance as inflation risks intensify and growth proves more resilient than expected.

item.title: What Trump’s Venezuela Blueprint Means for Cuba
item.description: Can Cuba withstand mounting US pressure? On the Big Take: the campaign to cripple Cuba’s government and why US intervention in Venezuela may be a blueprint for Havana’s future.

item.title: Uruguay Extends Monetary Pause, Holding Key Rate at 5.75%
item.description: Uruguay’s central bank kept its benchmark interest rate unchanged at 5.75% for a second consecutive policy meeting while also warning that the conflict in the Middle East has increased the upside risk to inflation.

item.title: Enhanced Games Parent Sinks After Troubled Debut Event
item.description: Enhanced Group Inc. shares fell to an all-time low after its inaugural sports-entertainment event was marred by technical problems and questions over its only world-record performance, disappointing investors weeks after its public listing.

item.title: Why Indonesia’s $15 Billion Free Lunch Program Is Unnerving Investors
item.description: On the Big Take Asia podcast: What it takes to feed tens of millions of Indonesians and why Prabowo’s signature program is under pressure.

item.title: Jefferies Says Investors Boost ‘Nuclear Exposure’: ESG Investing
item.description: Almost two-thirds of fund managers permit some level of “nuclear exposure,” with 34% allowing investments in nuclear weaponry, according to Jefferies Financial Group Inc.’s fourth-annual ESG and defense survey.

item.title: Stephen Jen Sees Yen Strengthening, Putting Carry Trades at Risk
item.description: Japan’s currency is poised to strengthen against the US dollar, reversing months of underperformance and threatening to upend a popular trade that relies on a weak yen, according to Eurizon SLJ Capital’s Stephen Jen.

item.title: Insurance Mogul Greg Lindberg Gets 12 Years for $2 Billion Fraud
item.description: North Carolina investment firm founder Greg Lindberg was sentenced Tuesday to 12 years in prison for siphoning more than $2 billion in reserves backing insurance policies and using the proceeds to pay for jets, mansions and a 214-foot yacht, according to defense lawyers.

item.title: Russia Weighs Curbing Diesel, Jet Fuel Exports as Attacks Grow
item.description: Russia is considering limiting exports of diesel and jet fuel, according to Interfax, as refinery run rates fall to multi-year lows amid Ukraine’s escalating attacks.

item.title: Croatia Seriously Considering Zigman as New Central Bank Chief
item.description: Croatia is seriously considering Ante Zigman to head its central bank, according to a person familiar with the government’s thinking.

item.title: Trump Is Pushing Mexico’s Export Profile Back to Low Value Goods
item.description: US tariffs pushed by President Donald Trump and supply-chain disruptions are eroding Mexico’s gains as a manufacturing hub, raising the risk that the country slips back into exporting lower-value goods that create few jobs or investment opportunities.

item.title: Volvo Car Avoids US Ban on Connected Vehicles Tied to China
item.description: The Trump administration reached an agreement with Volvo Car AB that will allow the automaker to avoid a US ban on connected vehicles tied to China.

item.title: Short Seller Left Testifies His Trades Matched Public Comments
item.description: Short seller Andrew Left told jurors in his securities fraud trial that his public comments about Tesla Inc. and other companies featured in his indictment never contradicted the short or long positions he took in their stocks, rebutting a key element of the government’s criminal case.

item.title: The Bond Selloff Is About More Than Inflation
item.description: A higher real rate regime.

item.title: MSG Sports Shares Rally to Record as Knicks Reach NBA Finals
item.description: Madison Square Garden Sports Corp. surged to a record on Tuesday, lifted by investor enthusiasm after the New York Knicks clinched their first NBA Finals berth in nearly three decades.

item.title: Apple, Google Blast Canada’s Plan to Expand Police Data Powers
item.description: A proposed law in Canada allowing police more access to citizens’ data faces a growing chorus of criticism from technology companies, including threats that they’ll pull out of the country.

item.title: Two More Oil Supertankers Exit Hormuz to Help Push Up Flows
item.description: Commercial shipping through the Strait of Hormuz gained modest momentum over the past 24 hours as at least two non-Iranian supertankers exited the Persian Gulf, the latest mini-flurry in energy flows transiting the vital waterway.

item.title: Webster Investors Approve $12 Billion Purchase by Santander
item.description: Webster Financial Corp.’s shareholders accepted the $12 billion takeover proposal from Banco Santander SA, clearing one of the last remaining hurdles for the deal.

item.title: Citadel Securities Warns That Fed Risks ‘Falling Behind Curve’
item.description: The Federal Reserve should shift closer toward hiking interest rates as rising consumer prices become the dominant threat to the economy, according to Citadel Securities.

item.title: KNDS on Track For Initial Public Offering as Sales Surge
item.description: KNDS NV said it’s pushing ahead with plans to go public as the Franco-German tank maker seeks to capitalize on surging demand that drove earnings and sales last year.

item.title: Rocket, Satellite Stocks Surge as SpaceX IPO Fuels Euphoria
item.description: Space and satellite-related stocks soared Tuesday as investor enthusiasm around the industry intensified after Elon Musk’s SpaceX filed for a public offering last week.

item.title: Dudley Says Fed Credibility at Risk on Years of Missing 2% Goal
item.description: Former New York Federal Reserve President Bill Dudley warned the US central bank is in danger of losing credibility as an inflation fighter after a longstanding failure to reach its 2% goal.

item.title: Micron Shares Rally, UBS Projects $1.8 Trillion Market Value
item.description: Micron Technology Inc.’s market capitalization, which has soared about 700% over the past year, will more than double over the next 12 months, according to at least one analyst.

item.title: American Airlines to Equip 500 Jets With SpaceX Starlink Wi-Fi Starting 2027
item.description: American Airlines Group Inc. plans to install SpaceX’s Starlink satellite internet service across more than 500 narrowbody aircraft beginning in early 2027, deepening an industrywide race among carriers to offer faster and more reliable onboard connectivity as they compete for premium travelers.

item.title: Qualcomm Strikes AI Chip Deal With TikTok Owner ByteDance
item.description: Qualcomm Inc. reached a deal with TikTok owner ByteDance Ltd. to supply chips for artificial intelligence data centers, according to people familiar with the matter, marking a key win for a company trying to expand from smartphone processors into AI infrastructure.

item.title: US Consumer Confidence Slips Amid Widespread Price Worries
item.description: US consumer confidence edged down in May as views of current economic conditions settled back amid rising prices due to the war in Iran.

item.title: Musk’s xAI Warns Staffers to Limit Contact With Cursor Employees
item.description: Employees at Elon Musk’s xAI were warned by the company’s top lawyer to carefully moderate their interactions with workers from Cursor — a directive that came weeks after Musk’s firm announced a possible deal to acquire the AI coding startup.

item.title: Revel to Merge With EQT-Backed Voltera, Uniting EV Charging Networks
item.description: Private equity-backed Voltera and Revel Transit Inc. have agreed to merge their electric-vehicle charging businesses to serve ride-hail cars and robotaxis across urban areas in the US.

item.title: Altman-Backed Nuclear Firm Oklo Rises on US Reactor Fuel Program
item.description: Oklo Inc. shares jumped after the the Sam Altman-backed reactor technology company said it’s in talks to participate in a US Department of Energy program to supply nuclear fuel, an effort aimed at relieving potential bottlenecks that threaten to slow the deployment of fission power.

item.title: Warner Boosts Loan Sale Again, Plans to Repay $15 Billion Bridge
item.description: Warner Bros. Discovery Inc. boosted a loan sale for a second time, a move that will allow the media giant to fully replace $15 billion of short-term financing.
